channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Family Court rejects sperm donor's bid to be named on child's birth certificate
item.description: Robert Albon, known online as Joe Donor, claims to have fathered 180 children through unregulated sperm donation methods advertised on social media. In a recent case, Albon sought to have his name on ...

item.title: Virginia Special Election Tests Voter Response to Proposed Democratic Redistricting
item.description: Virginia is holding a critical special election to decide on a Democratic-backed redistricting plan that could dramatically reshape the state's congressional map. If approved, the plan could provide D...

item.title: Huawei highlights AI-ready optical networks and their role in digital infrastructure
item.description: At the OptiX Club 2026 event in Johannesburg, Huawei outlined the growing importance of AI-ready optical networks for supporting data-heavy applications. Over 120 customers, partners, and industry sta...

item.title: Thom Tillis says he will block Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation until the Powell probe is resolved
item.description: The article focuses on Republican Sen. Thom Tillis of North Carolina and his decision to block Kevin Warsh’s path to becoming Federal Reserve chair. Tillis says he supports Warsh personally and consid...

item.title: Mum of Grade 10 Boy Who Cares for Siblings Opens Up on Family's Struggles
item.description: Ruth Maleve, a mother from Kayole, has shared her family's heartbreaking story after her Grade 10 son, Tito Wambua, took on the responsibility of caring for his younger siblings. In a moving video tha...

item.title: Amazon partners with Einride to deploy 75 electric heavy-duty trucks in U.S. logistics network
item.description: Amazon has entered into an agreement with Swedish freight technology company Einride to introduce 75 electric heavy-duty trucks into its U.S. logistics operations. Rather than purchasing or directly o...

item.title: Ex-official alleges Downing Street pressured Foreign Office over Mandelson US ambassador appointment
item.description: Sir Olly Robbins, a former senior Foreign Office official, has accused Downing Street of adopting a 'dismissive attitude' during the vetting process for Lord Mandelson's appointment as the UK's ambass...

item.title: Rising Kidney Disease in Africa Linked to Genetics, Lifestyle, and Limited Healthcare Access
item.description: Kidney disease is becoming a major health concern across Africa, with cases rising fast and many people not even aware they are affected until it is too late. The kidneys play a critical role in filte...

item.title: How Hunger, Stress, and Habit Influence Our Appetite
item.description: This article delves into the complex factors that influence our appetite, including hunger, stress, and habitual cues. Hunger is a biological signal regulated by the hypothalamus, which monitors blood...

item.title: Pakistan confirms adequate medicine stocks for next half year amid global challenges
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan’s Health Minister, Mustafa Kamal, announced that the country currently holds sufficient medicine stocks to meet the demand for the next five to six months. He mentioned that despit...

item.title: Richard Gadd’s Half Man explores male trauma and violence in an intense character drama
item.description: Richard Gadd’s drama Half Man is presented as a deeply unsettling and uncompromising exploration of male relationships, trauma, and violence. Structured across two timelines, the series opens with a t...

item.title: Iran's Participation in Second Round of Peace Talks with US Still Pending
item.description: Pakistan's Information Minister, Attaullah Tarar, confirmed that as of April 21, 2026, Iran has not yet formally responded regarding its participation in the second round of peace talks with the Unite...

item.title: Alan Osmond, member of The Osmonds, passes away at 76
item.description: Alan Osmond, best known as a member of the iconic pop group The Osmonds, has passed away at the age of 76. Throughout his career, Alan contributed significantly to the group's success, performing as b...

item.title: Exploring China-South Africa Collaboration in Space and Technology
item.description: In his recent address at the Green Tech Confucius Institute at the University of Venda in South Africa, Zou Song, a journalist from the Africa Center Branch of People’s Daily, explored the evolving te...

item.title: Pakistan calls for extension of US-Iran ceasefire amid ongoing diplomatic efforts
item.description: Pakistan has urged both the United States and Iran to extend their ongoing two-week ceasefire, stressing that dialogue and diplomacy remain the only workable path towards lasting peace in the region. ...

item.title: Olly Robbins testifies on pressure from No 10 over Peter Mandelson’s US ambassador appointment and vetting concerns
item.description: In a session before the House of Commons foreign affairs select committee, sacked senior civil servant Sir Olly Robbins provided evidence regarding the controversial appointment of Lord Peter Mandelso...

item.title: Gunman kills one and injures over a dozen at Mexico’s Teotihuacan pyramids
item.description: A tragic shooting occurred at the Teotihuacan archaeological site near Mexico City on Monday, resulting in the death of a Canadian woman and injuries to at least 13 others, including six Americans. Ac...

item.title: Over 1.9 Million in Cape Town Exposed to High Air Pollution Risks, Study Finds
item.description: A groundbreaking study from the University of Cape Town (UCT) reveals that over 1.9 million people in Cape Town are exposed to high to very high levels of air pollution, with the worst-hit areas locat...

item.title: Brighton aim to overtake Chelsea in Premier League clash with key player absences
item.description: Brighton face Chelsea at the Amex Stadium in a crucial Premier League match, with both sides aiming for a European spot next season. Brighton come into the game with momentum, following a 2-2 draw aga...

item.title: Afghan Man First Convicted Under UK’s New Channel Crossing Law
item.description: An Afghan man, Tajik Mohammad, has become the first person to be convicted under the UK's new law targeting dangerous crossings of the English Channel. Mohammad, aged 32, pleaded guilty at Canterbury ...
